data.title: Leidos upgraded at RBC on continued strength in defense, health
data.description:
Leidos Holdings (NYSE:LDOS) is little changed in Thursday's trading as RBC Capital upgraded shares to Outperform from Sector Perform with a $210 price target, raised from $180, citing continued strength and execution in the company's health services business, strong defense portfolio positioning, and increased confidence in the revised strategic direction under CEO Tom Bell.

Despite the stock's recent strength, RBC analyst Ken Herbert believes Leidos (NYSE:LDOS) will continue to beat earnings estimates and raise forecasts, with DOGE risks likely having troughed, and pointing to positive "balance sheet optionality," with M&A likely a focus.

Last month, Leidos (NYSE:LDOS) reported better than expected Q2 results and raised estimates for FY 2025 adjusted earnings to $11.15-$11.45/share from its prior forecast of $10.35-$10.75/share, while narrowing full-year revenue guidance to $17B-$17.25B from $16.9B-$17.3B previously.

The strength in Leidos' (LDOS) Health & Civil unit has been the main source of upside for the stock over the past year, benefiting from elevated veterans exam volumes and execution, Herbert said, expecting near-term volumes to support sustained margin strength.

data.title: TransMedics initiated with Hold at Stifel on mixed survey results
data.description:
• TransMedics (NASDAQ:TMDX) shares fell on Thursday after Stifel launched the organ care provider with a Hold recommendation, citing mixed results from a survey it conducted among 19 U.S. transplant surgeons.
  • The survey results indicated that organ utilization expansion should continue in the U.S. over the next few years despite a potential slowdown in donor growth, and rivals could pressure TransMedics’ (NASDAQ:TMDX) market share for its Organ Care System.
  • However, analyst Stephan Thomas argued that the company is positioned to benefit from attractive tailwinds in the near term (Next-Gen OCS Heart/Lung) and long term (OCS Kidney).
  • With a $155 price target on the stock, Thomas added that "we believe risk-reward is balanced at current levels when factoring in our mixed survey findings, which temper our confidence in meaningful upside potential to estimates.”

data.title: Wall Street trades higher ahead of Friday's payrolls report
data.description:
Wall Street trades higher on Thursday as investors weigh fresh jobs data for clues on the labor market’s strength.

The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) is +0.6%, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) is +0.5%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) is +0.6%.

On a sector-by-sector basis, eight of the 11 S&P segments are trading in positive territory, led by Consumer Discretionary and Financials. At the other end of the spectrum, Utilities has lagged the most on the session. 

In the bond market, yields are under some slight pressure as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) has dipped 3 basis points to 4.18%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) had pushed down 2 basis points to 3.59%.

On the economic front, job cuts climbed 39% in August, boosting the YTD total higher than all of 2024. Also, U.S. private sector employment continued softening, slightly more than expected, in August. 

Initial jobless claims rose slightly more than expected in the past week. Productivity was revised up, and labor costs were revised lower in second Q2 prints. 

The U.S. goods and services trade deficit widened more than expected in July.

Additionally, the U.S. ISM Services Index rose to 52.0 in August from 50.1 in July, exceeding the 50.9 consensus, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management

"The global bond selloff finally paused for breath yesterday, as weak U.S. data meant investors ramped up their expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts this year. The main catalyst was the JOLTS report for July, which showed that job openings fell to a 10-month low and exacerbated fears about a labor market slowdown," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

On more of the political side, President Donald Trump on Wednesday urged the Supreme Court to quickly accept and rule on an appeal aiming to overturn lower court rulings that deemed most of his tariffs illegal.

As for stocks that are on the move, shares of American Eagle Outfitters (AEO) have surged 34.8% after the apparel maker said the advertising campaign starring actress Sydney Sweeney gave its brand and business a reset.

data.title: Fiserv acquires mobile ordering and payment platform CardFree
data.description:
Fintech Fiserv (NYSE:FI) announced on Thursday the acquisition of CardFree, a mobile ordering and payment platform.

Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

data.title: RFK Jr. faces heat on Capitol Hill from an unexpected group: Senate Republicans
data.description:
While HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s appearance before the Senate Finance Committee Thursday was expected to generate fireworks from Democrats on the panel -- and it did -- three Repubican senators also took the secretary to task for his job so far.

The criticisms leveled by Sens. Bill Cassidy (La.), John Barrasso (Wyom.), and Thom Tillis (N.C.) are significant in that it shows there is frustration growing with RFK Jr. from GOP lawmakers. Barrasso is the second-highest-ranking Republican in the Senate.

Cassidy, by profession a medical doctor, had previously expressed some concern with RFK Jr. during his confirmation, though he ultimately voted for him. 

During the hearing, RFK Jr. said he agreed with Cassidy that President Trump should be nominated for the Nobel Prize for Operation Warp Speed, the public-private partnership that led to the development of COVID-19 vaccinations during Trump's first term. But Cassidy said he was perplexed that the secretary supports this given that as an attorney for anti-vaccine group Children's Health Defense, he wanted to restrict access to COVID shots. 

Cassidy also criticized the secretary for cutting $500M of funding toward the development of new mRNA vaccines. The Moderna (NASDAQ:MRNA) and Pfizer (NYSE:PFE)/BioNTech (NASDAQ:BNTX) COVID vaccines were created with the technology.

"It also seems like a commentary on what the president did during Operation Warp Speed which is to create a platform by which to create vaccines. So this just seems inconsistent that you agree that the president deserves a tremendous amount of credit for this."

Cassidy also took aim at RFK Jr.'s replacing all members of CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices, which the secretary did in June due to what he considered prior members' conflicts of interest. Cassidy said 6.9% of members had conflicts of interest, instead of the 97% alleged by RFK Jr. The senator added that some of the new members of that panel have served as expert witnesses in trials brought against vaccine manufacturers, which would appear to be a conflict of interest.

RFK Jr. responded by saying bias on that committee is OK "if that bias is disclosed."

Barrasso used his time to excoriate RFK Jr. on the mRNA vaccine research cuts, his handling of the measles outbreak in Texas and other southwestern states, as well as the ongoing turmoil at the CDC.

"In your confirmation hearings, you promised to uphold the highest standards for vaccines," the senator said. "Since then, I’ve grown deeply concerned."

Addressing the controversy over the firing of former CDC Director Susan Monarez, Tillis noted how the administration promoted her during her confirmation hearing only to have her dismissed a month into her tenure.

"I don’t see how you go over four weeks from a public health expert with unimpeachable scientific credentials, a longtime champion of MAHA values, caring and compassionate and brilliant microbiologist and four weeks later fire her."

Dear Readers: We recognize that politics often intersect with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Lifecore granted Sector Weight rating at KeyBanc on growth trajectory
data.description:
KeyBanc Capital Markets has initiated the coverage of Lifecore Biomedical (NASDAQ:LFCR) with a Sector Weight recommendation, noting a balanced outlook for the contract drug manufacturer’s growth prospects.

Analyst Paul Knight argued that Lifecore’s (NASDAQ:LFCR) focus, the fill/finish side of the business, is one of the fastest growing areas in contract manufacturing and one benefiting from the growth of biologics and the GLP-1 class of weight loss drugs.

Knight added that the company has also divested its non-core business, and a senior executive from Novo Holdings, the investment arm of Novo Nordisk's (NVO) controlling investor, has joined as its new VP of operations.

Amid capacity constraints for GLP-1s and a trend towards reshoring, “we believe these dynamics place LFCR in a position to win significant business in the coming years,” KeyBanc wrote, adding that the stock also trades at a relatively modest EV/sales ratio of 3.4x of its FY27 projections.

However, despite recent M&A deals such as Novo’s (NVO) acquisition of Catalent’s fill/finish sites, the analyst argued that Lifecore (NASDAQ:LFCR) will need to secure new programs or step up its contractual signups to achieve a meaningful multiple expansion.

data.title: Wells Fargo’s Paul Christopher: Expect choppiness ahead, trim positions
data.description:
Wells Fargo Investment Institute’s head of Global Market Strategy, Paul Christopher, is warning investors to prepare for potential market weakness amid rising inflation expectations.

In an interview with CNBC, Christopher expressed concern about recent increases in longer-dated inflation expectations, noting that “five-year inflation swaps have now ticked to highs for the year.”

The strategist pointed to growing market speculation around the Federal Reserve’s four-to-three majority composition and its potential impact on monetary policy.

“We do observe in the markets that there is some movement higher in longer dated inflation expectations, which indicates that markets are willing to at least price a possibility that the four-to-three majority could be decisive for maybe much lower rates in an extended way that could bring back inflation,” he said.

Christopher highlighted the concentration risk in today’s equity markets, where 10 stocks account for approximately 40% of the S&P 500’s (SP500) market capitalization. This concentration has created a pattern where “soft data pushes most of the stocks maybe a little bit lower, but then you get a good earnings report from one of those top 10, and it drags the market up higher,” resulting in choppy market conditions despite occasional record highs.

Given these conditions, the Wells Fargo strategist believes equity markets could be approaching a near-term top.

“We think there is the potential for some choppiness to continue and maybe turn into some more pronounced weakness, maybe a pullback 5-7% perhaps even 10%,” he cautioned, while recommending investors begin trimming positions.

For portfolio positioning, Christopher suggests maintaining an overweight to information technology (XLK) while reducing exposure to communication services (XLC) due to concentration concerns.

“Recycle those proceeds perhaps into some fixed income as a sort of a parking place while we get through the next few weeks,” he said.

data.title: Mega Matrix files $2B shelf for Ethena stablecoin token push
data.description:
Mega Matrix (NYSE:MPU) filed for a $2 billion shelf registration with the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Thursday to fund its new digital asset treasury (DAT) strategy.

The company aims to accumulate stablecoin governance tokens, especially Ethena’s ENA token, “to build the largest stablecoin governance token DAT company”.

“By building strategic positions, MPU gains both financial upside and a seat at the table where the future of money is being coded," the company added in its press release.

USDe, developed by Ethena, is supported by a combination of staked assets and liquid stablecoins like USDC and USDT, while its dollar peg is preserved through automated hedging in perpetual and futures markets, as per AInvest.

The move highlights the company’s pivot toward blockchain-driven financial solutions, with a particular focus on USDe- a delta-neutral

data.title: Carlyle Group raises $20B for secondary private equity assets
data.description:
Carlyle Group's (NASDAQ:CG) AlpInvest unit raised $20B for its global secondary strategies, with the final close of its AlpInvest Secondaries Fund VIII, which reached its $15B hard cap, the company said on Thursday. The capital raise represents a doubling of the company's investable capital in secondaries investments over its predecessor program. 

The fund is part of the firm's AlpInvest Secondaries Program VIII (ASP VIII). The total amount raised for the strategy includes $3.2B in co-investment commitments and $2B for private wealth vehicles investing alongside ASP VIII.

ASP VIII focuses on acquiring exposure to existing private equity assets by providing a range of liquidity solutions to general partners and limited partners, including the acquisition of fund interests, continuation funds, and other GP-centered solutions, Carlyle (NASDAQ:CG) said.

"This fundraise is not only a milestone for our secondaries strategy, but also a validation of the integrated platform we've built across Secondaries and Portfolio Finance,"  said Michael Hacker, partner and global head of Portfolio Finance at Carlyle AlpInvest. "With over $24 billion raised for liquidity solutions across our platform, we are well positioned to provide private equity sponsors and investors with a comprehensive toolkit of liquidity and capital solutions."

Carlyle (NASDAQ:CG) stock climbed 2.1% in midafternoon trading.

Carlyle AlpInvest has committed more than $40B to more than 245 transactions across secondaries and portfolio finance in the past 20 year. 

The global private equity investor also recently raised more than $4B for its portfolio finance strategies.
